generatedSentence: A solo hobbyist, known for his wish to explore uncharted jungles, set out in a kayak, collecting exotic fruit and precious keepsakes, noting the uncanny elegance of vines and the potency of a rare hickory figurine; his doghouse companion, a busybody jackass, caromed a loose nail safely past his ear lobes, admiring the hickory’s umber fabric art. Suddenly, a regiment of apache bandits, notorious for demolitions and illiquid loot acquisition, appeared, their blackjack dealings and trafficky reputation preceding them; the hero stiffened, an antibody of resolve forming, knowing they would oppose his ethical nonprofit business, and his earnings, once featured prominently, would be a tempting pitfall for their rogue union. The rogue leader, with a vexing eyebrow and an uncanny broadcasted charisma, challenged the hero's ontology, holding a hangman's rope and a loaded holster; a quick dunking of a runaway hippopotamus into the cannon-laden river caused immense turbulence, igniting the bamboo lumberyard and sending crisp, hot ash onto his leopard-print uniform, making the bandit utterly aback. Having successfully warded off the attack, the weary fellow, an egghead fanboy of strategy, sat by his kitchen window, contemplating his errand; the gambled victory allowed the vineyard to thrive, ratifying new distribution of its finest rarebit and crispy hamburger, while nearby, kewpies, flamingoes, and kakapo watched flights of rockets ascend, silencing the whispers of past warfare and leaving the bricked battlefield to the internal peace of the fasting juror.
panelSentences: A solo hobbyist, known for his wish to explore uncharted jungles, set out in a kayak, collecting exotic fruit and precious keepsakes, noting the uncanny elegance of vines and the potency of a rare hickory figurine; his doghouse companion, a busybody jackass, caromed a loose nail safely past his ear lobes, admiring the hickory’s umber fabric art.

Suddenly, a regiment of apache bandits, notorious for demolitions and illiquid loot acquisition, appeared, their blackjack dealings and trafficky reputation preceding them; the hero stiffened, an antibody of resolve forming, knowing they would oppose his ethical nonprofit business, and his earnings, once featured prominently, would be a tempting pitfall for their rogue union.

The rogue leader, with a vexing eyebrow and an uncanny broadcasted charisma, challenged the hero's ontology, holding a hangman's rope and a loaded holster; a quick dunking of a runaway hippopotamus into the cannon-laden river caused immense turbulence, igniting the bamboo lumberyard and sending crisp, hot ash onto his leopard-print uniform, making the bandit utterly aback.

Having successfully warded off the attack, the weary fellow, an egghead fanboy of strategy, sat by his kitchen window, contemplating his errand; the gambled victory allowed the vineyard to thrive, ratifying new distribution of its finest rarebit and crispy hamburger, while nearby, kewpies, flamingoes, and kakapo watched flights of rockets ascend, silencing the whispers of past warfare and leaving the bricked battlefield to the internal peace of the fasting juror.

The rogue leader, with a vexing eyebrow and an uncanny broadcasted charisma, challenged the hero's ontology, holding a hangman's rope and a loaded holster; a quick dunking of a runaway hippopotamus into the cannon-laden river caused immense turbulence, igniting the bamboo lumberyard and sending crisp, hot ash onto his leopard-print uniform, making the bandit utterly aback.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, cyberpunk graphic novel style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A lone explorer wearing a tattered fedora and a high-tech wrist gauntlet. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A lone figure in a kayak paddling through dense, glowing jungle vines, an exotic figurine clutched in one hand, viewed from a low angle. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A group of menacing, armored figures with glowing red eyes emerging from the jungle canopy, holding futuristic demolition charges, viewed from a medium shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The explorer, mid-action, orchestrating a giant hippopotamus's chaotic charge into a river full of floating cannon-like weapons, explosions of water and fire erupting, captured with a dynamic wide shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The triumphant explorer, now in a peaceful kitchen, looking out at a thriving vineyard with a rocket launching into the distance, framed in a close-up with cinematic lighting, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, NO text, NO speech bubbles, NO dialogue.

definitions.definition: Any projection or division, especially one of a somewhat rounded form.

In a subject view, or a world view, the set of conceptual or material things or classes of things that are recognised as existing, or are assumed to exist in context; in a body of theory, the ontology comprises the domain of discourse, the things that are defined as existing, together with whatever emerges from their mutual implications.
The theory of a particular philosopher or school of thought concerning the fundamental types of entity in the universe.

A cannon bit.

A carom.
definitions.example: In English billiards, a cannon is when one's cue ball strikes the other player's cue ball and the red ball on the same shot; and it is worth two points.

definitions.definition: The arm of a player that can throw well.
definitions.example: He's got a cannon out in right.

definitions.definition: A hollow cylindrical piece carried by a revolving shaft, on which it may, however, revolve independently.
(Chinese chess) A piece which moves horizontally and vertically like a rook but captures another piece by jumping over a different piece in the line of attack.
To bombard with cannons.
To play the carom billiard shot. To strike two balls with the cue ball
definitions.example: The white cannoned off the red onto the pink.

definitions.definition: To fire something, especially spherical, rapidly.
To collide or strike violently, especially so as to glance off or rebound.
A generally accepted principle; a rule.
definitions.example: The trial must proceed according to the canons of law.

definitions.definition: A group of literary works that are generally accepted as representing a field.
The works of a writer that have been accepted as authentic.
definitions.example: the entire Shakespeare canon

definitions.definition: A eucharistic prayer, particularly the Roman Canon.
A religious law or body of law decreed by the church.
definitions.example: We must proceed according to canon law.

definitions.definition: A catalogue of saints acknowledged and canonized in the Roman Catholic Church.
In monasteries, a book containing the rules of a religious order.
A member of a cathedral chapter; one who possesses a prebend in a cathedral or collegiate church.
A piece of music in which the same melody is played by different voices, but beginning at different times; a round.
definitions.example: Pachelbel’s Canon has become very popular.

definitions.definition: (Roman law) A rent or stipend payable at some regular time, generally annual, e.g., canon frumentarius
Those sources, especially including literary works, which are considered part of the main continuity regarding a given fictional universe.
definitions.example: A spin-off book series revealed the aliens to be originally from Earth, but it's not canon.

definitions.definition: A rolled and filleted loin of meat; also called cannon.
definitions.example: a canon of beef or lamb

definitions.definition: A large size of type formerly used for printing the church canons, standardized as 48-point.
The part of a bell by which it is suspended; the ear or shank of a bell.
A carom.
A clergy member serving a cathedral or collegiate church.
A canon regular, a member of any of several Roman Catholic religious orders.
A Near Eastern and Caucasian musical instrument related to the zither, dulcimer, or harp having either 26 strings and a single bridge, or twice that number and two bridges.
A person who is interested in an activity or a subject as a hobby.
definitions.example: This computer magazine is targeted at both professionals and hobbyists.

definitions.definition: An ethical drug, one only dispensed on the prescription of a physician.
Of or relating to the study of ethics.
definitions.example: The philosopher Kant is particularly known for his ethical writings.

definitions.definition: Of or relating to the accepted principles of right and wrong, especially those of some organization or profession.
definitions.example: All employees must familiarize themselves with our ethical guidelines.

definitions.definition: Morally approvable; good.
definitions.example: We are trying to decide what the most ethical course of action would be.

definitions.definition: (of a drug) Only dispensed on the prescription of a physician.
definitions.example: In most jurisdictions, morphine is classified as an ethical drug.

definitions.definition: In all likelihood.
A protein produced by B-lymphocytes that binds to a specific antigen.
A common gambling card game in casinos, where the object is to get as close to 21 without going over.
A hand in the game of blackjack consisting of a face card and an ace.
The flag (i.e., a jack) traditionally flown by pirate ships; popularly thought to be a white skull and crossed bones on a black field (the Jolly Roger).

A small, flat, blunt, usually leather-covered weapon loaded with heavy material such as lead or ball bearings, intended to inflict a blow to the head that renders the victim unconscious with diminished risk of lasting cranial trauma.
Any of several species of weed of genus Bidens, such as Bidens pilosa, in the family Compositae.
A blackjack oak.
To strike with a blackjack or similar weapon.
Characterised by traffic; having the noise and commotion of motor vehicles.
A kewpie doll.
To trouble aggressively, to harass.
To annoy, irritate.
To cause (mental) suffering to; to distress.
To twist, to weave.
To be irritated; to fret.
To toss back and forth; to agitate; to disquiet.
Vexation
A wading bird of the family Phoenicopteridae.
A deep pink color tinged with orange, like that of a flamingo.
A male fan who is obsessive about a particular subject (especially, something or someone in popular entertainment media).
To worship, support, drool over the object of affection; to be a fanboy.
A colleague or partner.
A companion; a comrade.
A man without good breeding or worth; an ignoble or mean man.
An equal in power, rank, character, etc.
One of a pair, or of two things used together or suited to each other; a mate.
A person with common characteristics, being of the same kind, or in the same group.
definitions.example: Roger and his fellow workers are to go on strike.

definitions.definition: A male person; a man.
A person; an individual, male or female.
A rank or title in the professional world, usually given as "Fellow".
(Aboriginal English) Used as a general intensifier
To suit with; to pair with; to match.
A person who was a fellow attendee at one's school.
definitions.example: I've lost touch with all my old schoolmates: I only see them at class reunions.

definitions.definition: The act of flying.
definitions.example: Most birds are capable of flight.

definitions.definition: An instance of flying.
definitions.example: The migrating birds' flight took them to Africa.

definitions.definition: The act of fleeing.
(collective) A collective term for doves or swallows.
definitions.example: a flight of swallows

definitions.definition: A trip made by an aircraft, particularly one between two cities or countries, which is often planned or reserved in advance.
definitions.example: The flight to Paris leaves at 7 o'clock tonight.

definitions.definition: A series of stairs between landings.
A group of canal locks with a short distance between them
A floor which is reached by stairs or escalators.
definitions.example: How many flights is it up?

definitions.definition: The feathers on an arrow or dart used to help it follow an even path.
A paper plane.
The movement of a spinning ball through the air - concerns its speed, trajectory and drift.
The ballistic trajectory of an arrow or other projectile.
An aerodynamic surface designed to guide such a projectile's trajectory.
An air force unit.
Several sample glasses of a specific wine varietal or other beverage. The pours are smaller than a full glass and the flight will generally include three to five different samples.
The shaped material forming the thread of a screw.
(of a spin bowler) To throw the ball in such a way that it has more airtime and more spin than usual.
(by extension) To throw or kick something so as to send it flying with more loft or airtime than usual.
The act of fleeing.
definitions.example: take flight

definitions.definition: A bald person, especially a man.
A bald head.
A coldly out of personal touch intellectual.
Any of several plants, native to mountain habitats, often grown in a rock garden
Of, relating to, or inhabiting mountains, especially above the timber line
definitions.example: alpine snows

definitions.definition: Of or relating to slalom and downhill skiing. (Compare Nordic.)
To take a risk, with the potential of a positive outcome.
To play risky games, especially casino games, for monetary gain.
To risk (something) for potential gain.
definitions.example: He gambled his reputation on the outcome.

definitions.definition: To interact with equipment at a casino
One thousand million (109) watts, abbreviated as GW.
Lacking liquidity; unable to be converted into cash.
definitions.example: Investment in illiquid assets may prove to be a mistake if the market turns down.

definitions.definition: To reprove in a formal or official way.
A rocket engine.
A non-guided missile propelled by a rocket engine.
A vehicle propelled by a rocket engine.
A rocket propelled firework, a skyrocket
An ace (the playing card).
An angry communication (such as a letter or telegram) to a subordinate.
A blunt lance head used in jousting.
Something that shoots high in the air.
A stupid or crazy person.
To accelerate swiftly and powerfully
To fly vertically
To rise or soar rapidly
To carry something in a rocket
To attack something with rockets
Welsh rarebit
Panthera pardus, a large wild cat with a spotted coat native to Africa and Asia, especially the male of the species (in contrast to leopardess).
(inexact) A similar-looking, large wild cat named after the leopard.
A lion passant guardant.
Any of various nymphalid butterflies of the genus Phalanta, having black markings on an orange base.
A facility dedicated to the preparation and/or sale of lumber.
definitions.example: He got a job at the lumberyard, working the big saw for planks.

definitions.definition: Near this place; nearby.
By this means, action or process.
definitions.example: I hereby declare you husband and wife.

definitions.definition: The sound of metal or glass clattering against itself.
definitions.example: He heard the jingle of her keys in the door and turned off the screen.

definitions.definition: A small piece of metal attached to a musical instrument, such as a tambourine, so as to make a jangling sound when the instrument is played.
definitions.example: Her tambourine didn't come with any jingles attached.

definitions.definition: A memorable short song, or in some cases a snippet of a popular song with its lyrics modified, used for the purposes of advertising a product or service in a TV or radio commercial.
definitions.example: That used-car dealership's jingle has been stuck in my head since we heard that song.

definitions.definition: A carriage drawn by horses.
A brief phone call; a ring.
definitions.example: Give me a jingle when you find out something.

definitions.definition: A jingle shell.
Coin money.
To make a noise of metal or glass clattering against itself.
definitions.example: The beads jingled as she walked.

definitions.definition: To cause to make a noise of metal or glass clattering against itself.
definitions.example: She jingled the beads as she walked.

definitions.definition: To rhyme or sound with a jingling effect.
To keep in safety, to watch over, to guard.
To defend, to protect.
To fend off, to repel, to turn aside, as anything mischievous that approaches; -- usually followed by off.
To be vigilant; to keep guard.
To act on the defensive with a weapon.
Of a lock: fitted with wards.
To transmit a message or signal through radio waves or electronic means.
To transmit a message over a wide area; specifically, to send an email in a single transmission to a (typically large) number of people.
To appear as a performer, presenter, or speaker in a broadcast programme.
To sow seeds over a wide area.
An executioner responsible for hanging criminals.
(games) A guessing game where one has to guess the word an opponent is thinking of by guessing one letter at a time, and involving the gradual drawing of a stick figure hanging from the gallows.
The use of a romantic and/or sexual relationship to entice a person into revealing secret information.
A case for carrying a tool, particularly a gun, safely and accessibly.
A belt with loops or slots for carrying small tools or other equipment.
To put something in a holster.
To restrict one’s personal consumption, generally of food, but sometimes other things, in various manners (totally, temporally, by avoiding particular items), often for religious or medical reasons.

definitions.definition: Abstinence from food
To estimate the value of; judge the worth of something.
definitions.example: I will have the family jewels valued by a professional.

definitions.definition: To fix or determine the value of; assign a value to, as of jewelry or art work.
To regard highly; think much of; place importance upon.
definitions.example: Gold was valued highly among the Romans.

definitions.definition: To hold dear.
definitions.example: I value these old photographs.

definitions.definition: A fool
(London and Essex) a large pickled gherkin or cucumber
(Of eyes) unusually pale; misaligned, sideways-looking, affected by strabismus.
To build with bricks.
To make into bricks.
To hit someone or something with a brick.
To make an electronic device nonfunctional and usually beyond repair, essentially making it no more useful than a brick.
definitions.example: My VCR was bricked during the lightning storm.

definitions.definition: The state or fact of being turbulent or agitated; tempestuousness, disturbance.
Disturbance in a gas or fluid, characterized by evidence of internal motion or unrest.
Specifically, a state of agitation or disturbance in the air which is disruptive to an aircraft.
An instance or type of such state or disturbance.
The internal workings of a mechanism or system that are normally hidden from view
To give formal consent to; make officially valid, sign off on.
The well-baked fat on the surface of a piece of roasted meat.
Having a crisp texture; brittle yet tender.
definitions.example: Baked rolls should be crispy.

definitions.definition: A mound, pile, or stack of stored hay.
(canoeing) A standing wave in a rapid.
The text string within which another string is searched for. (see: needle in a haystack)
A dish composed of a starchy food (rice, tortillas, crackers, etc.) topped by a protein (beans, cheese, meat, etc.) in combination with fresh vegetables, assembled on the plate by the diner.
A grape plantation, especially one used in the production of wine.
definitions.example: The vineyard of Château Margaux stands as the producer of one of the world's greatest and most sought-after red wines.

definitions.definition: The waging of war or armed conflict against an enemy.
Military operations of some particular kind e.g. guerrilla warfare.
To lead a military life; to carry on continual wars.
A potential problem, hazard, or danger that is easily encountered but not immediately obvious.
definitions.example: It's usually a simple task, but you should know the pitfalls before you attempt it yourself.

definitions.definition: A type of trap consisting of a concealed hole in the ground: victims fall into the hole and are unable to escape.
An antipattern.
